reasoning: The informal statement says that for any subgroup H of a group G, the subgroup generated by H equals H. The Lean signature takes an arbitrary `G : Type*` with a `Group G` instance and an arbitrary `H : Subgroup G`, and claims `Subgroup.closure H.carrier = H`. Here `H.carrier` is the underlying set of H, and `Subgroup.closure` produces the subgroup generated by a set — so `Subgroup.closure H.carrier` is exactly the subgroup generated by (the underlying set of) H, i.e., ⟨H⟩. This matches the standard Mathlib lemma `Subgroup.closure_eq : closure (K : Set G) = K`. No hypotheses are dropped or added, no types are silently changed, and the quantifier structure matches the informal "for any subgroup H of any group G". The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
